author: @kimkeam2094
text: I had just purchased a secondhand MTD Ride on mower and had to do exactly the same to remove the years of oil and muck that had built up and actually jammed and bent all the drive pulleys, gearbox etc. out of alignment. Three weeks later and it is fully restored. I suggest that you have a look for a service manual online as some parts may look the same prior to reassembly but when fitted can cause all sorts of grief. I look forward to seeing the results!

author: @RoadRageLive
text:
Love this idea. 

I made an electric lawn tractor about 5 years ago. I used an old Murray lawn tractor and a truck alternator as the motor with a brushless motor controller.  It worked very well for about a week and then caught fire. I now know that reusing the battery cables that came from the tractor was a mistake.  I have since purchase some 4 gauge jumper cables from a yard sale to replace the 6 gauge wires.  On the fire I also lost my 24 volt battery packs that I liberated from UPS units.  These batteries were ok but had very poor performance as they weren't made for such fast discharge.

My next plan is to replace the heavy lead acid batteries with lithium cells from laptops.  I have been collecting old laptop batteries for about a year now and almost have enough good cells to put together a battery.

author: @BiosWars
text: Some people are commenting about Rob not finishing projects. Understand, when your mind works in a great way, you always looks for better inexpensive solutions & ways to engineer different projects. Sometimes, and really most times, we find bites and bytes from the things we take apart, and those things help us finish those other past projects. This is actually the way that many great people work, including the late Nikola Tesla; I'm sure that even Elon Musk did this before he made his Billions.. Regardless, The Eco Guards in Canada work the same way, and we've been responsible for a great many inventions that many very large Corps have used and adopted world wide. It's not how or when you build it, as long as it eventually gets done. Furthermore, you try doing all this, without anyone's help. Keep Going and Doing Robert & Luke, you're both doing great and continually inspiring many.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I don't know why folks say I don't finish projects mate - as far as I am concerned they are finished - true enough they are not exhaustive - I just tend to take them to a stage where the improvements are obvious and the proof of concept is done - from that's it - I don't really like to flog dead horses lol  - so I am not going to spend time doing a project to death rom every angle lol

replies.author: @BiosWars
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering I Don't blame you guys, proof of concept helps those that want to pursue those types of projects on their own.  Just keep up the great work, I'm still being impressed & inspired. Jeff

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @BiosWars I think it is all about exploration mate - cheers
